title: castela tweediei var. typica
intro: Castela tweediei var. typica, also known as Castela, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Traditionally, Castela has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Castela include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. Castela can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized effectively.
